rulings.Case Title: EPIFANIO JOSE VENEGAS ET AL VS. ANSHUMAN S NADKARNI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Alternatively Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Dec. 27, 2019 line 1. DEFENDANT ANSHUMAN NADKARNI, RENUKA NADKARNI MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Alternatively Summary Adjudication is OFF CALENDAR, Notice of Settlement on file. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: OFELIA MUNGUIA ET AL VS. ARTURO RODRIGUEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Dec. 27, 2019 line 2. DEFENDANT ARTURO RODRIGUEZ MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND is DENIED. According to the Notice of Motion, the sole basis for relief is CCP 473. CCP 473 requires that request for relief shall be made within "reasonable time, in no case exceeding six months." This motion was filed on November 27, 2019. Further, Notice of Motion is seeking to vacate a default entered on "April 10, 2019." However, Defendant Rodriguez' default was entered on February 26, 2019, and based on a letter attached to Aldana's declaration, Aldana knew of this default as of March 7, 2019 at the latest. To the extent the motion is seeking equitable relief discussed in the Points and Authorities, such relief is not properly before the Court as it is not specified in the Notice of Motion. However, the Court will note that the basis for such relief is not supported by facts. Rodriguez was defaulted on February 26, 2019. Aldana claims that he was "under the impression that during his March 6, 2019 telephone conversation with Attorney Miller an extension was graciously granted." On March 6, 2019 there was no extension to have been granted as default had already been entered. Furthermore, no details are provided as to the length of such purported extension. The Court further notes that counsel again failed to comply with SFLR 2.7B (see May 24, 2019 Minute Order) and failed to provide courtesy copies of the moving papers. Failure to comply with SFLR in the future will result in motion being taken off calendar. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: 2255 MARKET, LLC VS. JAMILA AISHA ABNER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Pltf'S Second Mtn To Compel Jamila Aisha Abner'S Deposition, Req. For An Award Of Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Dec. 27, 2019 line 3. PLAINTIFF 2255 MARKET, LLC Ntc Of Mtn And Pltf'S Second Mtn To Compel Jamila Aisha Abner'S Deposition, Req. For An Award Of Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED, subject to opposition. Defendant shall appear and sit for a deposition the week of January 6, 2019. Plaintiff to serve a 5-day notice. Sanctions granted as requested. Hearing Required to address why Mr. Driscoll's December 18, 2019 e-mail should not be referred to the California State Bar for consideration. = (501/CFH)
